safe_default: Keep vanilla 26.2 height unless the player creates a new world with this preset or explicitly enables a height integration.

recommended_height_options.name: conservative
recommended_height_options.use_case: More room for sedimentary cover, deep ore belts and deepslate without heavily stressing compatibility.

recommended_height_options.name: realistic_beta
recommended_height_options.use_case: Clearer crustal layering for new test worlds; requires runtime validation of 26.2 dimension type and worldgen height codecs.

recommended_height_options.name: smart_core_linked
recommended_height_options.height_multiplier_source: config/xxsx_mc_smart_core-common.toml or a future shared config value
recommended_height_options.use_case: Optional integration with Smart Core's world-height UI. Earth on Minecraft should read a shared value or world preset selection, not hard-link to Smart Core Java classes.

planned_layers.name: weathered_and_lateritic_zone
contents: near-surface copper/gold traces when above hydrothermal belts

implementation_rules: Implement as a new-world preset or explicit config route, not as a default dimension override.
Before enabling, inspect vanilla 26.2 dimension_type/world preset JSON formats and runtime logs for RegistryDataLoader errors.
When height changes, retune every placed_feature height band together; otherwise deposits can vanish or crowd into the wrong layer.
If Smart Core is installed, integration should be optional and file/config based so Earth on Minecraft remains standalone.
